# Browse Sidebar Search and Filenames Implementation Plan

> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.

**Goal:** Add fzf-style fuzzy matching and readable long filenames to the browse mode sidebar.

**Architecture:** Keep the feature inside the existing browse shell generated by `scripts/lib/standalone-preview-server.js`. Add DOM-independent fuzzy helper functions to the inline script, route all filtering through those helpers, and adjust file-name rendering/styles without changing server APIs.

**Tech Stack:** Node.js, vanilla browser JavaScript, generated HTML/CSS, built-in `assert` tests.

---

## File Structure

- Modify: `scripts/lib/standalone-preview-server.js`
  - Export `buildBrowseShellHtml` for tests.
  - Add fuzzy search helpers inside the generated browser script.
  - Update search input and `openFile()` filtering to use the helper.
  - Render file names with optional matched-character spans.
  - Change `.file-name` CSS to two-line wrapping with stable width.
- Create: `test/browse-sidebar-search.test.js`
  - Extract the generated inline browser script.
  - Execute it in a lightweight VM with stubbed DOM globals.
  - Test fuzzy behavior through pure helper functions.
- Run existing: `test/browse-service.test.js`
  - Confirms browse service behavior remains unchanged.

### Task 1: Test Fuzzy Helper Behavior

**Files:**
- Create: `test/browse-sidebar-search.test.js`
- Modify: `scripts/lib/standalone-preview-server.js`

- [ ] **Step 1: Export shell builder for tests**

In `scripts/lib/standalone-preview-server.js`, change the module exports block at the end from:

```js
module.exports = {
  startStandalonePreviewServer,
};
```

to:

```js
module.exports = {
  buildBrowseShellHtml,
  startStandalonePreviewServer,
};
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Add failing test**

Create `test/browse-sidebar-search.test.js` with:

```js
const assert = require('assert')
const vm = require('vm')

const { buildBrowseShellHtml } = require('../scripts/lib/standalone-preview-server')

function createElement(tagName) {
  return {
    tagName,
    className: '',
    innerHTML: '',
    textContent: '',
    children: [],
    style: {},
    classList: {
      add() {},
      remove() {},
      contains() { return false }
    },
    appendChild(child) {
      this.children.push(child)
      return child
    },
    addEventListener() {},
    setAttribute() {},
    removeAttribute() {},
    querySelector() {
      return createElement('div')
    },
    querySelectorAll() {
      return []
    }
  }
}

function createContext() {
  const elements = {}
  const context = {
    console,
    URLSearchParams,
    localStorage: {
      getItem() { return null },
      setItem() {}
    },
    location: { hash: '' },
    window: {
      innerWidth: 1200,
      addEventListener() {}
    },
    document: {
      documentElement: { setAttribute() {}, style: {} },
      getElementById(id) {
        if (!elements[id]) elements[id] = createElement('div')
        return elements[id]
      },
      createElement,
      addEventListener() {}
    },
    fetch() {
      throw new Error('fetch should not be called by fuzzy helper tests')
    }
  }

  context.document.getElementById('search-input').value = ''
  context.document.getElementById('preview-frame').contentWindow = { postMessage() {} }
  context.window.document = context.document
  context.globalThis = context
  return context
}

function runBrowseScript() {
  const html = buildBrowseShellHtml()
  const match = html.match(/<script>([\s\S]*)<\/script>\s*<\/body>/)
  assert.ok(match, 'browse shell should include an inline script')

  const context = createContext()
  vm.createContext(context)
  vm.runInContext(match[1], context)
  return context
}

const context = runBrowseScript()

assert.strictEqual(typeof context.fuzzyMatchEntryName, 'function')
assert.strictEqual(typeof context.filterEntriesByQuery, 'function')

const nonContiguous = context.fuzzyMatchEntryName('fop', 'file-open-options.md')
assert.strictEqual(nonContiguous.matched, true)
assert.deepStrictEqual(nonContiguous.indices, [0, 5, 10])

const entries = [
  { name: 'file-open-options.md', relativePath: 'a', kind: 'file' },
  { name: 'foo.md', relativePath: 'b', kind: 'file' },
  { name: 'feature-overview-plan.md', relativePath: 'c', kind: 'file' }
]

const filtered = context.filterEntriesByQuery(entries, 'foo')
assert.deepStrictEqual(filtered.map((item) => item.entry.name), [
  'foo.md',
  'file-open-options.md',
  'feature-overview-plan.md'
])
assert.deepStrictEqual(filtered[0].match.indices, [0, 1, 2])

const empty = context.filterEntriesByQuery(entries, '')
assert.deepStrictEqual(empty.map((item) => item.entry.name), entries.map((entry) => entry.name))
assert.strictEqual(empty[0].match, null)

const miss = context.filterEntriesByQuery(entries, 'zz')
assert.deepStrictEqual(miss, [])

process.stdout.write('browse-sidebar-search tests: ok\n')
```

### Task 2: Implement Fuzzy Filtering and Highlight Data

**Files:**
- Modify: `scripts/lib/standalone-preview-server.js`
- Test: `test/browse-sidebar-search.test.js`

- [ ] **Step 1: Add pure fuzzy helpers inside the inline script**

Insert after `function buildParentPath(rel) { ... }`:

```js
    function fuzzyMatchEntryName(query, name) {
      var needle = String(query || '').toLowerCase().trim();
      var haystack = String(name || '').toLowerCase();
      if (!needle) {
        return { matched: true, indices: [], score: 0 };
      }

      var indices = [];
      var searchFrom = 0;
      var score = 0;
      var previousIndex = -1;

      for (var i = 0; i < needle.length; i++) {
        var foundAt = haystack.indexOf(needle.charAt(i), searchFrom);
        if (foundAt === -1) {
          return { matched: false, indices: [], score: -Infinity };
        }

        indices.push(foundAt);

        if (foundAt === i) score += 12;
        if (foundAt === 0 || /[\\s._\\-/]/.test(haystack.charAt(foundAt - 1))) score += 8;
        if (previousIndex !== -1 && foundAt === previousIndex + 1) score += 10;
        score -= foundAt - searchFrom;

        previousIndex = foundAt;
        searchFrom = foundAt + 1;
      }

      score -= haystack.length * 0.01;
      return { matched: true, indices: indices, score: score };
    }

    function filterEntriesByQuery(entries, query) {
      var q = String(query || '').trim();
      if (!q) {
        return entries.map(function(entry, index) {
          return { entry: entry, match: null, index: index };
        });
      }

      return entries
        .map(function(entry, index) {
          return {
            entry: entry,
            match: fuzzyMatchEntryName(q, entry.name),
            index: index
          };
        })
        .filter(function(item) { return item.match.matched; })
        .sort(function(left, right) {
          if (right.match.score !== left.match.score) {
            return right.match.score - left.match.score;
          }
          return left.index - right.index;
        });
    }
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Run fuzzy test to verify it passes**

Run:

```bash
node test/browse-sidebar-search.test.js
```

Expected: PASS and prints `browse-sidebar-search tests: ok`.


### Task 3: Wire Filtering Into the Sidebar UI

**Files:**
- Modify: `scripts/lib/standalone-preview-server.js`
- Test: `test/browse-sidebar-search.test.js`

- [ ] **Step 1: Update search input handler**

Replace the current search input listener:

```js
    searchInput.addEventListener('input', function() {
      var q = searchInput.value.toLowerCase().trim();
      if (!q) {
        renderFileList(allEntries);
        return;
      }
      var filtered = allEntries.filter(function(e) {
        return e.name.toLowerCase().indexOf(q) !== -1;
      });
      renderFileList(filtered);
    });
```

with:

```js
    searchInput.addEventListener('input', function() {
      renderFileList(filterEntriesByQuery(allEntries, searchInput.value));
    });
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Normalize render input and add filename renderer**

At the start of `renderFileList(entries)`, add:

```js
      var items = entries.map(function(item, index) {
        if (item && item.entry) return item;
        return { entry: item, match: null, index: index };
      });
```

Then replace all later uses of `entries` in that function with `items`, and set `var entry = item.entry` inside the loop.

Add this helper before `renderFileList()`:

```js
    function renderFileName(target, name, match) {
      target.textContent = '';
      target.title = name;
      if (!match || !match.indices || !match.indices.length) {
        target.textContent = name;
        return;
      }

      var matched = {};
      match.indices.forEach(function(index) {
        matched[index] = true;
      });

      for (var i = 0; i < name.length; i++) {
        var node = document.createElement(matched[i] ? 'mark' : 'span');
        if (matched[i]) node.className = 'file-name-match';
        node.textContent = name.charAt(i);
        target.appendChild(node);
      }
    }
```

Replace:

```js
        nameSpan.textContent = entry.name;
```

with:

```js
        renderFileName(nameSpan, entry.name, item.match);
```

- [ ] **Step 3: Update selected-file rerendering**

Replace the `renderFileList(allEntries.filter(...))` call inside `openFile()` with:

```js
      renderFileList(filterEntriesByQuery(allEntries, searchInput.value));
```

### Task 4: Improve Long Filename Display

**Files:**
- Modify: `scripts/lib/standalone-preview-server.js`
- Test: `test/browse-sidebar-search.test.js`

- [ ] **Step 1: Replace file-name CSS**

Replace:

```css
    .file-name { display: block; overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; white-space: nowrap; }
```

with:

```css
    .file-name {
      display: -webkit-box;
      max-height: 36px;
      overflow: hidden;
      line-height: 18px;
      overflow-wrap: anywhere;
      -webkit-line-clamp: 2;
      -webkit-box-orient: vertical;
    }
    .file-name-match {
      border-radius: 2px;
      background: var(--accent-soft);
      color: var(--accent);
      font-weight: 700;
    }
```
